author: yf225
text: In-place operation on differentiable view leaks memory

author: yf225
text:
```
import torch
import gc

gc.collect()
print(torch.cuda.memory_allocated(0))  # prints 0

m = torch.nn.Linear(2,3)
mw = m.weight[:]
m.to('cuda')
# with torch.no_grad():  # Without using `torch.no_grad()`, the next line will leak CUDA memory
mw[0][0] = 5

del mw
del m
gc.collect()
print(torch.cuda.memory_allocated(0))  # prints 512
```

This is reproducible on master.

Initial investigation shows that the leak is not because of saving Variables for backward (the SavedVariable constructor was not called).

cc [@ezyang](https://github.com/ezyang) [@ssnl](https://github.com/ssnl) [@albanD](https://github.com/albanD) [@zou3519](https://github.com/zou3519) [@gqchen](https://github.com/gqchen) [@ngimel](https://github.com/ngimel)

author: albanD
text:
This is a ref-cycle in cpp that goes as `x -> CopySlices -> AccumulateGrad -> x`.  
This only happens because `x` here is a leaf Tensor.  
Note that after doing such thing, trying to run backward will fail with "leaf variable has been moved into the graph interior" so we should fail earlier before the cycle is created.  
This can be done by making [this check](https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/blob/6249d7302b7277864ed0ade93f58d88ee0cd3aa8/torch/csrc/autograd/VariableTypeUtils.h#L44) detect views of leafs in addition to only leafs.

author: albanD
text: Note that fixing [#3818](https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/3818) would also fix this issue as breaking the `AccumulateGrad -> x` link will solve this as well.
